backlog.title: Bug Fix Suite — Isolation & Correctness Hardening
backlog.description: Fix three validated defects in agent-browser-pool: (1) Major — reaper's unanchored pgrep/pkill -f kills other lanes' live Chromes; (2) Minor — doctor hard-FAILs on missing optional `ss`; (3) Minor — pool_ensure_connected reconnect/relaunch paths skip the BUG-1 identity check. All three are in lib/pool.sh (disjoint functions, no inter-issue code dependencies). Each fix includes a regression test (implicit TDD) and inline doc updates. A final doc-sync task sweeps README.md and skill docs.

milestones.title: Issue Fixes & Regression Tests
milestones.description: Implement all three fixes with regression coverage. Issues are independent (disjoint functions) but applied to the same source tree sequentially. Tests follow the single-setup selftest runner pattern (AGENTS.md §4: setup() called at most once, bodies run in the main shell).

tasks.title: Fix reaper unanchored pgrep/pkill -f (Issue 1 — Major isolation violation)
tasks.description: The orphan-dir sweep in pool_reap_orphan_dirs uses pgrep/pkill -f -- "user-data-dir=$dir" which matches as a regex SUBSTRING. Lane 3's pattern is a substring of lane 30's cmdline, so reaping orphan lane 3 kills live lane 30 (and 31-39, 300, etc.). Fix by anchoring the pattern to the lane-dir boundary. Add a regression test that spawns fake-Chrome processes on prefix-colliding lane numbers.

subtasks.title: Anchor pgrep/pkill pattern in pool_reap_orphan_dirs + regression test for prefix collision
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/recon_issue1_reaper.md and architecture/system_context.md. The bug is at lib/pool.sh:2898-2902 inside pool_reap_orphan_dirs (function spans 2874-2912). The current code is:
     if pgrep -f -- "user-data-dir=$dir" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         pkill    -f -- "user-data-dir=$dir" 2>/dev/null || true
         sleep 0.2
         pkill -9 -f -- "user-data-dir=$dir" 2>/dev/null || true
     fi
   where $dir="$POOL_EPHEMERAL_ROOT/$base" (e.g. /home/.../active/3). pgrep/pkill -f match the pattern as a regex SUBSTRING of /proc/<pid>/cmdline. Lane 3's pattern (…/active/3) is a substring of lane 30 (…/active/30), 31, 300, etc. — so reaping orphan lane 3 kills all those live lanes' Chromes. The only caller is pool_admin_reap (lib/pool.sh:3959), reachable via the user-facing `agent-browser-pool reap` command.
2. INPUT: No data dependencies from other subtasks. This is a standalone fix to pool_reap_orphan_dirs. The function reads $POOL_EPHEMERAL_ROOT and calls pool_lease_exists("$base") to identify orphans (dirs with no lease).
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §h3.0 (Issue 1) fix.
   (a) CODE FIX: Anchor the pgrep/pkill pattern to the lane-dir boundary so it matches ONLY the exact dir followed by a space (next flag) or end-of-line. Replace all three occurrences of "user-data-dir=$dir" with an anchored ERE. The recommended approach (PRD suggested fix):
       local pat="user-data-dir=$dir( |$)"
       if pgrep -f -- "$pat" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
           pkill    -f -- "$pat" 2>/dev/null || true
           sleep 0.2
           pkill    -9 -f -- "$pat" 2>/dev/null || true
       fi
   Chrome's cmdline is `--user-data-dir=<dir>` followed by either a space (next --flag) or end-of-line, so `( |$)` after the dir defeats the prefix collision. $dir is absolute and $base is validated ^[0-9]+$, so the regex is injection-safe. The `.` in the path is a regex metachar but matches itself (permissive, not unsafe).
   (b) COMMENT UPDATE: The inline comment at ~line 2895-2897 says 'FULL ABSOLUTE --user-data-dir path so a different lane's Chrome is never hit' — this was FALSE (the bug). Update it to explain the anchoring: e.g. 'Anchored to the lane-dir boundary with ( |$) so a prefix-colliding lane (e.g. lane 30 when reaping lane 3) is never hit — the OLD unanchored substring match killed every lane whose number started with the orphan's.'
   (c) REGRESSION TEST: Add a new selftest function `selftest_reap_orphan_dirs_kills_only_target_lane` in test/validate.sh. It will be auto-picked-up by the single-setup _run_selftest_suite runner (compgen -A function | grep '^selftest_'). The test must:
     - Run in an isolated timeout-bounded bash subshell (pattern: write a body.sh, source pool.sh, AGENT_CHROME_EPHEMERAL_ROOT redirected to temp, timeout 15). Mirror selftest_doctor_flags_disconnected_lease (test/validate.sh:852-881) for the hermetic subshell pattern.
     - Create ephemeral dirs for lanes 3 and 30 ($EPHEMERAL_ROOT/3, $EPHEMERAL_ROOT/30).
     - Spawn TWO fake-Chrome processes with controlled argv using setsid + sleep:
         setsid -f sleep 300 -- --user-data-dir=$EPHEMERAL_ROOT/3 --remote-debugging-port=53423  &
         setsid -f sleep 300 -- --user-data-dir=$EPHEMERAL_ROOT/30 --remote-debugging-port=53453  &
       (or a small wrapper script that exec's sleep with the argv). Capture both PIDs.
     - Make lane 3 an orphan (no lease) and lane 30 leased (call pool_lease_write for lane 30).
     - Call pool_reap_orphan_dirs.
     - Assert: lane 3's process is DEAD (pgrep -f its exact argv finds nothing, or /proc/$pid3 gone) AND lane 30's process is ALIVE (/proc/$pid30 exists).
     - CLEANUP: kill lane 30's surviving fake-Chrome process (kill -- -$pgid30 then wait $pid30) before returning. Never leak processes (AGENTS.md §3).
4. OUTPUT: pool_reap_orphan_dirs with the anchored pattern (function behavior unchanged for non-colliding lanes; only the false-positive kills are eliminated). New selftest_ function that proves isolation. The function's contract (echo orphan count to stdout, rc 0 always) is unchanged.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comment in pool_reap_orphan_dirs (lib/pool.sh ~2895-2897) to document the anchoring fix. No README change needed for this subtask — the README §reap description ('killing any orphaned Chrome still pointed at them') remains accurate post-fix.

tasks.title: Fix doctor ss optional-dep false-FAIL (Issue 2 — Minor)
tasks.description: pool_admin_doctor puts `ss` in the same FAIL loop as required deps, but ss is optional (its absence degrades pool_find_free_port to a curl-only probe). On a host without ss, doctor falsely exits 1. Fix by moving ss to an optional block mirroring the notify-send pattern, and update the docstring.

subtasks.title: Move ss from FAIL loop to optional block + update doctor docstring + add ss-missing test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/recon_issue2_doctor.md and architecture/system_context.md. The bug is in pool_admin_doctor (lib/pool.sh:4231-4488). The dependencies loop at lib/pool.sh:4258 is:
     for dep in flock setsid pgrep pkill cp curl jq findmnt ss; do
         if command -v "$dep" >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf '  %-22s OK\n' "$dep"; ok=$((ok+1))
         else printf '  %-22s MISSING\n' "$dep"; fail=$((fail+1)); fi
     done
   `ss` is in this loop but its own inline comment (lib/pool.sh:4255-4257) says 'no FAIL, no WARN'. The runtime caller pool_find_free_port (lib/pool.sh:1412-1416) degrades silently: `listeners="$(ss -tlnH 2>/dev/null || true)"`. The reference pattern for optional deps is notify-send (lib/pool.sh:4292-4299): a separate if-block with MISSING printed but NO fail++.
   IMPORTANT: `findmnt` STAYS in the FAIL loop — its absence causes pool_check_btrfs to fail. Only `ss` moves out.
2. INPUT: No data dependencies from other subtasks. Standalone fix to pool_admin_doctor.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §h3.1 (Issue 2) fix.
   (a) CODE FIX — remove ss from the loop:
       Change `for dep in flock setsid pgrep pkill cp curl jq findmnt ss; do`
       to    `for dep in flock setsid pgrep pkill cp curl jq findmnt; do`
   (b) CODE FIX — add a dedicated ss block AFTER the loop (before or right after the Chrome check, still within [dependencies]). Mirror notify-send (lib/pool.sh:4292-4299):
       # ss — OPTIONAL (pool_find_free_port degrades to curl-only probe when absent).
       # Absence is NOT a FAIL — port allocation still works via the live curl probe.
       if command -v ss >/dev/null 2>&1; then
           printf '  %-22s OK\n' "ss"
           ok=$((ok+1))
       else
           printf '  %-22s MISSING (optional; port-probe degrades to curl-only)\n' "ss"
       fi
   (c) DOCSTRING UPDATE — make the severity model internally consistent:
       - lib/pool.sh:4169 '(not counted): notify-send MISSING (optional).' → add: '; ss MISSING (optional, degrades to curl-only port probe).'
       - lib/pool.sh:4176 OUTPUT line 'MISSING = FAIL, except notify-send' → 'MISSING = FAIL, except notify-send and ss'
       - lib/pool.sh:4148 the `a.` bullet listing required deps → add ss to the OPTIONAL list alongside notify-send.
       - lib/pool.sh:4178 [dependencies] OUTPUT section → add an `ss` row mirroring the notify-send row: 'ss  OK | MISSING (optional; port-probe degrades to curl-only)'
   (d) TEST: Add selftest_doctor_ss_optional_when_missing in test/validate.sh (hermetic timeout-bounded subshell pattern, mirrors selftest_doctor_flags_disconnected_lease at test/validate.sh:852-881). Stub `command -v ss` to fail (override the bash builtin: `ss() { return 127; }` won't work for `command -v ss`; instead use `unpath_ss` by creating a PATH that lacks ss, OR override the loop's check by stubbing the function `command() { [[ "$2" == "ss" ]] && return 1; builtin command "$@"; }`). Assert: the [dependencies] section shows 'ss MISSING (optional', and the FAIL count is NOT incremented by ss (the exit code may still be 1 from the tmpfs-non-btrfs FAIL on the temp tree, so assert specifically that 'ss' line contains 'optional' and does not appear as a bare 'MISSING' without the qualifier). Keep it simple: assert the ss line contains 'optional'.
4. OUTPUT: pool_admin_doctor with ss as a non-counted optional dependency. The [summary] exit code is now driven only by genuinely-blocking failures (flock/setsid/pgrep/pkill/cp/curl/jq/findmnt/chrome/binary/btrfs/master). New selftest function.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the pool_admin_doctor docstring (severity model + OUTPUT contract + the `a.` bullet) as detailed in step (c) above. These are inline docstrings in lib/pool.sh — they ride WITH the code fix.

tasks.title: Harden pool_ensure_connected identity check on both binding paths (Issue 3 — Minor)
tasks.description: pool_ensure_connected is the per-call hot path but skips the BUG-1 identity check (pool_cdp_is_ours) that the acquire path (_pool_launch_and_verify) was hardened with. The reconnect branch rebinds the daemon to anything answering CDP on the port; the relaunch branch calls pool_wait_cdp with a single arg (identity disabled). Fix both paths so a foreign Chrome on the lane's port is never silently driven.

subtasks.title: Add BUG-1 identity gate to reconnect branch + fix broken existing test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/recon_issue3_ensure_connected.md and architecture/system_context.md. pool_ensure_connected spans lib/pool.sh:2508-2625.
   CURRENT jq extraction (lib/pool.sh:2534):
     mapfile -t _f < <(jq -r '.session, .port, .ephemeral_dir, .connected' <<<"$json")
     session="${_f[0]:-}"; port="${_f[1]:-}"; ephemeral_dir="${_f[2]:-}"; connected="${_f[3]:-true}"
   CURRENT reconnect branch (lib/pool.sh:2561-2572):
     if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$port/json/version" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         if pool_daemon_connect "$session" "$port"; then … return 0; fi
         … return 1
     fi
   BUG: after curl succeeds, pool_daemon_connect rebinds WITHOUT verifying the answerer is this lane's Chrome. A foreign Chrome on the port → silent isolation break.
   The identity function is pool_cdp_is_ours PORT USER_DATA_DIR EXPECTED_PID (lib/pool.sh:1629-1652): checks DevToolsActivePort first-line == PORT AND /proc/$EXPECTED_PID exists. Returns 0=ours, 1=not provably ours (NON-FATAL).
   CRITICAL TEST IMPACT: selftest_ensure_connected_rebinds_when_disconnected (test/validate.sh:560-588) stubs curl()→0 (reconnect branch) with chrome_pid=200 (dead, no DevToolsActivePort file). After the fix, pool_cdp_is_ours returns 1 → falls through to relaunch instead of reconnecting → the test BREAKS. It MUST be updated.
2. INPUT: No dependencies on other subtasks. Standalone fix to pool_ensure_connected's reconnect branch.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §h3.2 (Issue 3) reconnect-branch fix.
   (a) EXTRACT chrome_pid FROM LEASE: Modify the jq extraction at lib/pool.sh:2534 to also read chrome_pid:
       mapfile -t _f < <(jq -r '.session, .port, .ephemeral_dir, .connected, .chrome_pid' <<<"$json")
       (add a 5th field). Then: chrome_pid="${_f[4]:-}". Coalesce null/empty to 0: `[[ "$chrome_pid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || chrome_pid=0`.
   (b) ADD IDENTITY GATE IN RECONNECT BRANCH: After the curl succeeds (lib/pool.sh:2561), before calling pool_daemon_connect, verify identity:
       if curl -sf "http://127.0.0.1:$port/json/version" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
           # BUG-1 identity hardening (Issue #3): verify the answerer is OUR Chrome.
           # When we have a valid chrome_pid, gate the reconnect on pool_cdp_is_ours.
           # A foreign Chrome answering our port (narrow race: daemon restart + our
           # Chrome dead + foreign Chrome grabbed the port) must NOT be rebound.
           if [[ "$chrome_pid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$chrome_pid" -gt 0 ]] \
               && ! pool_cdp_is_ours "$port" "$ephemeral_dir" "$chrome_pid"; then
               _pool_log "pool_ensure_connected: lane $lane foreign Chrome on port $port → relaunch"
               # Fall through to the relaunch branch below (do NOT rebind to foreign Chrome).
           elif pool_daemon_connect "$session" "$port"; then
               pool_lease_update "$lane" connected true
               pool_lease_update "$lane" last_seen_at "$now"
               _pool_log "pool_ensure_connected: lane $lane reconnected (same chrome, port=$port)"
               return 0
           else
               _pool_log "pool_ensure_connected: lane $lane reconnect FAILED (chrome alive, connect rc 1)"
               pool_lease_update "$lane" last_seen_at "$now"
               return 1
           fi
       fi
       KEY DESIGN: The guard `[[ "$chrome_pid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$chrome_pid" -gt 0 ]]` means: if we have NO valid pid (old lease / provisional), skip the identity check and preserve legacy behavior (connect to whatever answers). Only when we HAVE a valid pid do we enforce identity. This avoids breaking lanes with stale chrome_pid=0.
   (c) FIX BROKEN EXISTING TEST: Update selftest_ensure_connected_rebinds_when_disconnected (test/validate.sh:560-588). It stubs curl()→0 with chrome_pid=200. Two options:
       Option A (preferred): stub pool_cdp_is_ours to return 0 (ours) so the reconnect branch fires as intended: `pool_cdp_is_ours() { return 0; }`.
       Option B: create a fake DevToolsActivePort file ($ephemeral_dir/DevToolsActivePort with the port as first line) + use pid 1 (init, always alive at /proc/1) as chrome_pid.
       Either way the test must still assert _connect_called==1 and connected flipped to true.
   (d) NEW REGRESSION TEST: Add selftest_ensure_connected_rejects_foreign_chrome_on_reconnect. Hermetic timeout-bounded subshell (mirrors selftest_doctor_flags_disconnected_lease pattern). Setup: write a lease with connected=false, valid chrome_pid (e.g. 1 = init), a port. Stub: curl()→0 (something answers), pool_daemon_connected()→1 (daemon doesn't know session), pool_cdp_is_ours()→1 (NOT ours — foreign Chrome). Assert: pool_daemon_connect is NOT called (daemon NOT bound to foreign Chrome), and pool_ensure_connected falls through (return non-zero or reaches relaunch). This proves the identity gate prevents binding to a foreign Chrome.
4. OUTPUT: pool_ensure_connected with identity-gated reconnect branch. Existing test fixed. New regression test. The function's contract (return 0=connected, 1=not) is unchanged; the behavioral change is: foreign Chrome on reconnect → fall through to relaunch instead of silent rebind.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the comment block above the reconnect branch (lib/pool.sh ~2559-2560) to document the identity gate. No docstring change needed here (the pool_wait_cdp/pool_cdp_is_ours docstring update is in S2).

subtasks.title: Enable identity args in relaunch branch pool_wait_cdp + update pool_wait_cdp/pool_cdp_is_ours docstrings
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/recon_issue3_ensure_connected.md. The relaunch branch of pool_ensure_connected is at lib/pool.sh:2574-2625.
   CURRENT relaunch wait call (lib/pool.sh:2597) — SINGLE ARG, identity DISABLED:
     if ! pool_wait_cdp "$port"; then
         … pool_lease_update "$lane" connected false; return 1
     fi
   The hardened reference is _pool_launch_and_verify (lib/pool.sh:2297-2347) which ALWAYS passes 3 args:
     pool_wait_cdp "$port" "$ephemeral_dir" "${POOL_CHROME_PID:-}"
   pool_wait_cdp (lib/pool.sh:1697-1741): check_identity is enabled only when BOTH user_data_dir (absolute path) AND expected_pid (numeric) are supplied. With identity enabled, after curl succeeds it calls pool_cdp_is_ours; mismatch → keep polling (30s budget); timeout → kill pgroup, return 1.
   The vars needed are ALREADY IN SCOPE: $ephemeral_dir (extracted from lease, line 2536) and ${POOL_CHROME_PID:-} (set by pool_chrome_launch at line 2584, written to lease at 2593). So the fix is changing ONE line's arity.
   DOCSTRING IMPACT: pool_wait_cdp docstring (lib/pool.sh:1689) says 'the ensure_connected relaunch path, which already knows its Chrome is bound' — this is now FALSE after the fix. pool_cdp_is_ours docstring (lib/pool.sh:1622) has a similar statement about 'the ensure_connected relaunch path' using legacy behavior. Both must be updated.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T3.S1 (which modified the jq extraction to add chrome_pid — S2's relaunch branch is in the same function and must coexist with S1's changes). The relaunch branch follows the reconnect branch in the same function; S1's fall-through logic delivers control here when the reconnect identity check fails.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §h3.2 (Issue 3) relaunch-branch fix.
   (a) CODE FIX — enable identity args (lib/pool.sh:2597):
       Change:  if ! pool_wait_cdp "$port"; then
       To:      if ! pool_wait_cdp "$port" "$ephemeral_dir" "${POOL_CHROME_PID:-}"; then
   This enables the BUG-1 identity check inside pool_wait_cdp: after curl succeeds on the relaunched Chrome, pool_cdp_is_ours verifies DevToolsActivePort == port AND /proc/$POOL_CHROME_PID exists. If a foreign Chrome grabbed the port (our Chrome died on EADDRINUSE), the identity check fails → pool_wait_cdp keeps polling → times out → kills our pgroup → returns 1 → connected:false (correct: we do NOT bind to the foreign Chrome).
   (b) BEHAVIORAL NOTE: If POOL_CHROME_PID is empty (defensive edge), pool_wait_cdp's check_identity guard rejects it (not ^[0-9]+$), so identity stays disabled — legacy behavior preserved. In practice POOL_CHROME_PID is always set after pool_chrome_launch.
   (c) DOCSTRING UPDATE — pool_wait_cdp (lib/pool.sh ~1687-1689):
       Remove or revise: 'the ensure_connected relaunch path, which already knows its Chrome is bound'
       Replace with: 'the ensure_connected relaunch path now also passes identity args (Issue #3 fix), so all pool_wait_cdp call sites enforce identity when a pid is available. The legacy probe-only behavior is preserved ONLY for standalone tests.'
   (d) DOCSTRING UPDATE — pool_cdp_is_ours (lib/pool.sh ~1620-1623):
       Remove or revise: 'back-compat for standalone tests / the ensure_connected relaunch path'
       Replace with: 'back-compat for standalone tests (the ensure_connected relaunch path was hardened in the Issue #3 fix and now passes identity args).'
   (e) TEST: The relaunch-branch fix is covered by the existing pool_wait_cdp identity tests in test/validate.sh (selftest at ~706-763, which stub pool_wait_cdp for _pool_launch_and_verify). For the ensure_connected relaunch path specifically, add or extend a test: hermetic subshell, stub pool_chrome_launch (sets POOL_CHROME_PID), stub pool_wait_cdp to verify it receives 3 args (record $2/$3), assert the relaunch branch passes identity args. If S1's regression test already exercises the fall-through-to-relaunch path, this may be a lightweight assertion.
4. OUTPUT: pool_ensure_connected relaunch branch with identity-enabled pool_wait_cdp. Updated docstrings for pool_wait_cdp and pool_cdp_is_ours. The function's contract is unchanged.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the pool_wait_cdp docstring (lib/pool.sh ~1687-1689) and pool_cdp_is_ours docstring (lib/pool.sh ~1620-1623) to remove the now-false claim about the ensure_connected relaunch path. These ride WITH the code fix.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final sweep of changeset-level docs (README.md, skill docs) to ensure they accurately reflect the three fixes. Per the DOC SYNC rule §5 Mode B: cross-cutting overview docs get a final pass. Each implementing subtask already updated its inline comments/docstrings (Mode A); this task handles README-level summaries.

subtasks.title: Sweep README.md and skill docs for accuracy post-fix
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md Documentation Surface table. The three fixes are in lib/pool.sh (inline comments + docstrings already updated by their implementing subtasks via Mode A). This task handles ONLY changeset-level overview docs.
2. INPUT: All three fixes are complete (dependencies on P1.M1.T1.S1, T2.S1, T3.S1, T3.S2). Review the final state of lib/pool.sh to confirm what changed, then cross-reference against the docs.
3. LOGIC: Review and update the following doc files IF they are now inaccurate:
   (a) README.md §doctor (around line 225-240): The [dependencies] section currently lists 'flock, setsid, pgrep, pkill, cp, curl, jq, chrome → OK / MISSING; notify-send → OK / MISSING (optional)'. After Issue 2, `ss` also shows as optional. CONSIDER adding `ss` to the optional list: 'notify-send, ss → OK / MISSING (optional)'. Also verify the line 'Exits 0 if healthy, 1 only if a blocking infrastructure check fails' is now MORE accurate (ss absence no longer triggers exit 1).
   (b) README.md §reap (around line 188-200): Verify the description 'killing any orphaned Chrome still pointed at them' is still accurate (it is — the fix changes HOW the kill is scoped, not the behavior description). Likely NO change needed, but verify.
   (c) README.md §Architecture/Internals (around line 300-320): The line 'pool_ensure_connected (reconnect if the daemon died)' at line 320 — CONSIDER whether to mention the identity hardening. Likely no change needed (it's a high-level summary), but verify it's not misleading.
   (d) README.md §Dependencies (around line 47-48): 'A handful of coreutils/util-linux/procps tools are also required (flock, setsid, pgrep, pkill, cp, curl, jq; notify-send is optional).' — ss is NOT listed here. CONSIDER adding 'ss' as optional alongside notify-send. This is a judgment call: ss is part of iproute2 and almost always present; mentioning it as optional is accurate but may clutter. Recommend: add a brief note.
   (e) .agents/skills/agent-browser-pool/SKILL.md and references/configuration.md: Check if they mention reaper behavior, doctor dependencies, or ensure_connected identity. Update ONLY if they make claims contradicted by the fixes.
   IMPORTANT: If a doc file is ALREADY accurate, do NOT change it. This is a verification + targeted update, not a rewrite. If no changeset-level doc needs updating, document that finding (each file checked + verdict) and mark the subtask complete.
4. OUTPUT: Updated (or verified-accurate) README.md and skill docs. A brief note in the PRP/research of what was checked and what (if anything) changed.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task. No further doc subtask needed.
